content.problemStatement:
You are given a **simple undirected connected graph** with NNN nodes (numbered 111 to NNN) and MMM edges.

Chef is initially located at vertex SSS, and a robot is located at vertex A0(A0≠S)A_0 (A_0 \ne S)A0​(A0​=S). The robot is programmed to move along a specific path, while Chef is trying to avoid meeting with the robot.

There is a period of KKK days, and the robot is programmed with a sequence of K+1K + 1K+1 integers - A0,A1,…,AKA_0, A_1, \ldots, A_KA0​,A1​,…,AK​ such that on the iii-th day, the robot moves from node Ai−1A_{i - 1}Ai−1​ to node AiA_iAi​. Here, AiA_iAi​ must be adjacent to Ai−1A_{i - 1}Ai−1​.

Chef is aware about the sequence AAA and can construct his plan appropriately to counter the program of the robot. Every day, he can do one of the following actions:


Choose to remain in the same node.
Choose to move to an adjacent node.
Chef is said to meet the robot if either of the following condition holds:


At the end of any day, Chef and the robot are in the same node.
During any day, Chef and robot move along the same edge (crossing in opposite directions).

For example, Chef is going from node 222 to node 333 and the robot is going from node 333 to node 222 (on the same day).
**All other scenarios are not considered as meeting the robot.** Notably, it is possible for Chef to go from node 111 to node 222 at the same time as the robot is going from node 222 to node 333, or vice versa.

A sequence AAA is called avoidable if it is possible for Chef to construct a plan and avoid meeting the robot at all times.

Your task is to count the number of avoidable sequences. Note that unlike Chef's starting city SSS, the variable A0A_0A0​ is not fixed for the robot, and we have to consider all possibilities of A0A_0A0​.

Also, remind yourself of the constraints that A0≠SA_0 \ne SA0​=S and AiA_iAi​ adjacent to Ai−1A_{i - 1}Ai−1​.

Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 444 integers - N,M,KN, M, KN,M,K and SSS.
The next MMM lines contain 222 integers each - uuu and vvv, representing an edge.




Output Format
For each test case, output the number of avoidable sequences modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The graph is connected and simple (no multi-edges or self-edges)
The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases both do not exceed 300030003000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The only valid sequence according to the constraints is (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1) but that is not avoidable because Chef cannot stay in node 111 (same node condition), or go to node 222 (not crossing condition).
Test Case 2 : There are 333 sequences to be considered (2,1),(2,3)(2, 1), (2, 3)(2,1),(2,3) and (3,2)(3, 2)(3,2) of which (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) and (3,2)(3, 2)(3,2) are avoidable.
